J1.basis: The business pivoted from **InboxZap** (an email inbox-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant)... incomplete across the rest of the site and, critically, **incomplete in the source repo itself**... GSC exports confirm the pivot broke organic performance: virtually all historical impression volume is tied to the dead product's keywords
J1.rationale: Nails all three surfaces of anchor_10: live=meeting assistant, GSC=legacy inbox demand, repo=partially stale, and every recommendation is framed around that reality (e.g. 'no organic footprint in its actual category').

J2.basis: 301-redirect the 12 legacy InboxZap post URLs (`/blog/post-1` … `/blog/post-12`) to `/blog`... A 301 to the blog hub preserves whatever residual link equity exists... Expect the ~13.8k impressions... to mostly disappear
J2.rationale: Treats the 12 as one data-justified tier (all off-topic → 301 to hub) with real aggregate GSC numbers and reasoning, but never walks them asset-by-asset or differentiates by per-post impressions, and redirects everything to the hub rather than to relevant successors.

J3.basis: that traffic was never going to convert for the current product, and holding onto it was actively costly... A 301... preserves whatever residual link equity exists... target 1,500+ words... in a category the site has zero footprint in today... Monitor GSC after the legacy-post redirects go live
J3.rationale: Explicitly names the tension and lands a defensible middle: 301 to preserve residual equity rather than torch it, build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and sequences/monitors the transition — avoiding both failure modes.

J1.basis: The business pivoted from **InboxZap** (email cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), but the pivot was incomplete in ways actively hurting organic traffic... GSC data shows correlates with terrible rankings (~13.8k impressions, ~6 clicks, positions 42–58)
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly labels the inbox-cleanup content/queries as dead-product legacy, flags the repo as stale vs. live, and frames every fix around the meeting-assistant reality.

J2.basis: 301-redirected all 12 legacy InboxZap post URLs to `/blog` (documented reasoning...) Expect the ~13.8k impressions currently going to these URLs to mostly disappear
J2.rationale: Grounds the decision in the real GSC numbers (13.8k impressions, 6 clicks, pos 42–58) and assigns an explicit, justified disposition, but applies one blanket 301-to-hub to all 12 rather than a per-asset or tiered walk checking whether any individual post had salvageable equity worth a targeted redirect or harvest.

J3.basis: A 301 to the blog hub preserves whatever residual link equity exists and removes the mismatch... build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and sequence the transition
J3.rationale: Names the tension explicitly, rejects both failure modes (won't fake-repurpose off-brand content, won't just torch equity), preserves residual link equity via 301, and stands up the new meeting-AI cluster as the growth engine with a monitoring window — a defensible, sequenced middle path.
